title: Hanging car sachet
subtitle: A small hanging fabric sachet filled with dried botanicals and a few drops of essential oil for the car
excerpt: A small hanging sachet of dried lavender, cedar shavings, and a few drops of essential oil provides a gentle, natural alternative to synthetic car air fresheners. Hung from the rear-view mirror or placed on a seat, it releases fragrance slowly over several weeks. Refresh by adding a few more drops of essential oil when the scent fades.
sourceNotes: Dried lavender and cedar as natural fragrance materials: standard sachets and pomanders reference. Cedar shavings (from untreated red cedar: Juniperus virginiana or Thuja plicata) release cedrene and related compounds that act as a mild insect deterrent as well as a fragrance. Refreshing fabric sachets with essential oil: standard aromatic craft reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Make several sachets in a single session; each lasts 3 to 6 weeks before needing refreshing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Sachets can be made in advance and stored in a sealed bag until needed, which concentrates the fragrance.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: The sachet filling lasts 6 months. When the fragrance fades (usually after 3 to 6 weeks), add 2 to 3 drops of essential oil directly onto the sachet and the scent will return. Replace the filling after 6 months or when it no longer responds to refreshing with more oil.

glossaryTerms.definition: A material that slows the evaporation of volatile fragrance compounds and extends how long a scent lasts. Cedar shavings act as a mild fixative in this sachet.

content.text: Commercial car air fresheners typically use synthetic fragrance compounds suspended in a gel or card. This sachet uses dried lavender, cedar shavings, and essential oil for a softer, more natural scent. Cedar shavings act as a
that slows the release of the lavender fragrance and adds depth. The sachet can be refreshed with drops of essential oil when the fragrance fades, extending its life considerably.

items.prepNote: untreated cedar; from craft or pet bedding suppliers
to boost the fragrance; or peppermint, eucalyptus, or cedarwood

content.text: Add the lavender buds and cedar shavings to a small muslin or cotton drawstring bag. Add the essential oil drops and toss to mix. Tie the bag closed. For a hanging sachet, thread a length of ribbon or twine through the drawstring to hang from the rear-view mirror.

When the fragrance fades (usually after 3 to 6 weeks), add 2 to 3 drops of essential oil directly onto the outside of the bag and knead gently to distribute. The scent will return within a few hours as the oil warms in the car. Replace the entire filling after 6 months.
Sachets stored unused in a sealed bag keep 6 months before the fragrance starts to dissipate. In use, refresh with essential oil every 3 to 6 weeks. Replace the filling after 6 months.
